questions.question: 中文导读：For matrix multiplication (m x n) @ (n x p), what must be true about the dimensions?
options: 中文选项：m must equal p
中文选项：The inner dimensions n must match
中文选项：All dimensions must be equal
中文选项：m must be greater than p
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Matrix multiplication requires the number of columns in the first matrix (n) to equal the number of rows in the second matrix (n). The result has shape (m x p).

questions.question: 中文导读：What is the identity matrix?
options: 中文选项：A matrix of all ones
中文选项：A square matrix with ones on the diagonal and zeros elsewhere that acts as the multiplicative identity
中文选项：A matrix where every element is unique
中文选项：The transpose of any given matrix
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The identity matrix I has ones on the diagonal and zeros everywhere else. Multiplying any matrix by I returns the original matrix unchanged, like multiplying a number by 1.

questions.question: 中文导读：What is the key difference between element-wise multiplication and matrix multiplication?
options: 中文选项：Element-wise is faster while matrix multiplication is more accurate
中文选项：Element-wise multiplies matching positions (same shape required), matrix multiplication takes dot products of rows and columns (inner dimensions must match)
中文选项：They produce the same result but use different notation
中文选项：Element-wise only works on vectors while matrix multiplication works on matrices
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Element-wise (Hadamard) product multiplies corresponding elements and requires identical shapes. Matrix multiplication computes dot products between rows and columns with the rule (m,n)@(n,p)=(m,p).

questions.question: 中文导读：In the expression 'output = relu(W @ x + b)', what role does broadcasting play?
options: 中文选项：It broadcasts the computation across multiple GPUs
中文选项：It automatically stretches the bias vector b to match the shape of W @ x so they can be added
中文选项：It converts the data types of W and x to match
中文选项：It repeats the relu activation across all elements
questions.explanation: 中文解析：W @ x produces a column vector, and b is also a vector. Broadcasting stretches b across the batch dimension if needed, allowing element-wise addition without explicit shape matching.

questions.question: 中文导读：What does a determinant of zero for a matrix indicate?
options: 中文选项：The matrix has all zero entries
中文选项：The matrix is singular: it crushes at least one dimension, cannot be inverted, and has no unique solution
中文选项：The matrix is the identity matrix
中文选项：The matrix performs a rotation
questions.explanation: 中文解析：A zero determinant means the transformation collapses space by at least one dimension (e.g., mapping 2D to a line). The matrix has no inverse, and linear systems using it have either no solution or infinitely many.
